input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: When your Reasoning mappings identify a performer/entity named in the passages who plausibly fills the role queried (e.g., the passages attribute the song to 'Don Henley') but your output does not yet state whether that performer’s membership/role in the band named in the question is verified.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing per-passage mapping behavior: quote or paraphrase supporting text from each passage, map each quoted fact to ENTITY and SCOPE, and cite passage indices. Preserve the final decision flow that either extracts a final answer when an exact supporting-text pattern is present or emits a retrieval plan when it is not.
delta_p.change_rule: After listing per-passage mappings, append a single concise 'Bridge / missing fact and next-hop' sentence that (1) names the bridge entity in canonical/title-first form (use the exact entity string as it already appears in passages, e.g., 'Don Henley'), (2) states precisely which fact needs confirmation for the question (e.g., 'membership in the Eagles'), and (3) supplies a prioritized next-hop retrieval directive that: names the retrieval target in canonical form ('Don Henley'), gives at least three short disambiguating query variants (ordered by priority) where the first variant begins with the canonical name, includes one-sentence rationale linking the target to the bridging passage index, and specifies the exact supporting-text pattern required from a retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern). Keep this appended content to one compact paragraph (no multi-sentence plans). Example required pattern format: "Don Henley (.*) (member|former member|lead vocalist|drummer) of the Eagles". Restrict suggested sources to authoritative biography/profile sites (e.g., Wikipedia, AllMusic, Rolling Stone) when listing queries.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not produce a final answer unless the exact required supporting-text pattern is present in the provided passages. Do not invent or assume membership. Do not repeat long verbatim spans from the passages beyond short quoted substrings already used in mappings. Avoid adding extra retrieval variants beyond the required three prioritized short queries. Do not change the overall output section formatting requirements in the base prompt.
delta_p.output_contract: The output must still follow the base prompt format: include the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] block with per-passage mappings, then either (a) final-answer extraction or (b) the retrieval plan if the exact pattern is absent. If the appended bridge/next-hop directive was triggered, the one-line [[ ## summary ## ]] must either present the cited answer plus passage indices (if present) or a single-line retrieval statement of the form: 'retrieve [Don Henley]' (optionally followed by the top query variant).
delta_p.why_local_transition: This minimal rule forces the agent to expose the specific missing verification (band membership) and give a compact, prioritized next-hop retrieval direction so the pipeline can perform the second hop; it leaves existing mapping and extraction behavior unchanged.

delta_p.trigger: When the agent is producing a first-hop summary that links the song title in the passages to a performer (e.g., passages mention 'The End of the Innocence' and 'Don Henley') and the current summary does not explicitly frame the bridge as a membership-verification need or recommend biography-style sources for the next hop.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep all existing summary behavior: map quoted/paraphrased passage text to entities and scopes, cite passage indices, avoid answering from memory, and do not invent facts. Preserve the required output structure and the 'summary' field interface.
delta_p.change_rule: Adjust the wording and focus of the first-hop summary to (a) express the attribution succinctly as an observed linkage from the song page to the named performer, (b) expose the bridge entity explicitly as the performer's canonical name, and (c) state the missing fact as a membership-verification question framed 'whether <PERFORMER> is/was a member of the Eagles'. Also recommend a next-hop retrieval direction that targets biography or authoritative profile pages (examples: Wikipedia, AllMusic, Rolling Stone) and ask specifically to verify 'Eagles membership and years'. Do not assert the membership as fact or provide additional biographical details.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or infer the performer's Eagles membership; do not add years, dates, or other facts not present in the provided passages; avoid copying long unseen spans outside the visible passages; and avoid replacing the existing citation-style evidence mapping required by the base prompt.
delta_p.output_contract: When triggered, the agent's 'summary' output must (1) state a concise first-hop attribution linking the song to the performer (citing the passage indices used), (2) expose the bridge entity by name and specify the single missing fact as 'whether <PERFORMER> is/was a member of the Eagles', and (3) give a next-hop retrieval direction that names biography/profile sources (e.g., Wikipedia, AllMusic, Rolling Stone) and requests verification of 'Eagles membership and years'. The output must keep citation of passages and must not include a final answer or invented facts.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This edge only requires a small, targeted shift in phrasing and retrieval-focus: make the bridge explicit as a membership-verification task and recommend biography-type sources. That concentrates the next-hop without changing the agent's larger mapping and citation behavior.

delta_p.trigger: When the current reasoning identifies a bridge entity (a person or named entity extracted from the first-hop passages, e.g. 'Don Henley') but also concludes that the provided passages do NOT contain an explicit statement of that entity's membership/association with the target organization (here: the Eagles).
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing output format and all required behavior: produce the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] and [[ ## summary ## ]] blocks; continue to quote/paraphrase passages, map each quoted fact to ENTITY and SCOPE and cite passage indices; preserve the rule to refuse final answering if the exact supporting text is absent and to emit a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan.
delta_p.change_rule: When emitting the prioritized next-hop retrieval plan, replace vague multi-source suggestions with a concise, canonical target and tightly specified retrieval requirements: (1) Name the target entity in canonical/title-first form: 'Don Henley'. (2) Give a one-sentence rationale linking that target to the question and cite the bridging passage index (e.g., the passage that identifies the song as by Don Henley). (3) Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants, ordered by priority; ensure the first variant begins with the canonical/title-first form ('Don Henley ...'). Include scope/context tokens (e.g., 'membership', 'Eagles', 'biography', 'years'). (4) Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer: a sentence explicitly containing both the token sequence 'Don Henley' and an explicit membership phrase containing 'member of the Eagles' or 'former member of the Eagles' (optionally accompanied by years as YYYY–YYYY or the phrase 'from YEAR to YEAR'). (5) Provide explicit bridging phrase(s) to include in the follow-up query (e.g., 'The End of the Innocence' + 'Don Henley' or 'Don Henley' + 'Eagles'). (6) Briefly justify the need for a second hop by citing the passage indices that show the song→Don Henley connection but no explicit membership statement.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not broaden the retrieval plan by listing many example websites or by permitting answers from memory. Avoid vague instructions like 'check authoritative profiles' without naming the canonical target. Do not instruct the agent to answer the question without retrieving the explicit supporting-text pattern above.
delta_p.output_contract: After this edit, whenever the agent issues a 'missing' next-hop retrieval plan it must: name 'Don Henley' as the target entity (canonical-first), cite the bridging passage index, give a one-sentence rationale, produce ≥3 prioritized short query variants (first beginning with 'Don Henley'), state the exact literal supporting-text pattern required (must include 'Don Henley' + an explicit 'member of the Eagles' phrase or equivalent, optionally with years in YYYY–YYYY or 'from YEAR to YEAR'), supply bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up query, and include a one- or two-sentence reasoning justification citing passage indices. The rest of the summary format and mapping requirements remain unchanged.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This is a small, local refinement: it narrows a previously vague 'retrieve biography or authoritative profiles' instruction to a single canonical retrieval target and an exact textual pattern needed to close the hop. That minimal tightening improves the follow-up retrieval's precision while preserving all existing mapping, citation, and format behavior.

delta_p.trigger: When the reasoning identifies a bridge entity extracted from the passages (e.g., the passages name 'Don Henley') and the reasoning concludes the exact answer is not yet supported by the provided passages.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the current behavior of quoting/paraphrasing supporting text from each passage and mapping each quoted fact to ENTITY and SCOPE. Preserve the rule to refuse a final answer when the exact required supporting-text pattern is not present in the given passages.
delta_p.change_rule: Augment the missing-hop retrieval-plan behavior only: in the 'If missing' section, require the agent to (1) name the canonical/title-first target entity to retrieve (use the bridge entity as the canonical title, e.g., 'Don Henley'), (2) give a one-sentence rationale linking that entity to the question and cite the bridging passage index, (3) provide at least three ordered, concise query variants for the follow-up retrieval (the first variant must begin with the canonical/title-first form), (4) specify the exact literal supporting-text pattern that will permit a final answer from the retrieved document — a sentence that contains the canonical name and an explicit statement of membership with the band (examples of acceptable literal patterns: "Don Henley ... member of the Eagles", "Don Henley ... founding member of the Eagles", or "Don Henley ... was a member of the Eagles"), and (5) list explicit bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include (e.g., "Don Henley" + "Eagles" or "Don Henley" + "band membership"). Do not add extra steps or change other sections.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or extract a final answer without a retrieved document that matches the exact supporting-text pattern. Avoid inventing biography details (membership years, titles) unless they appear verbatim in retrieved text. Do not output full biography passages — only the required supporting sentence pattern from retrieved documents should be cited when present.
delta_p.output_contract: Outputs must still follow the base format ([[ ## reasoning ## ]] then [[ ## summary ## ]]). If the exact supporting text is absent, the summary line must name the canonical target and state 'retrieve [canonical target]' (optionally listing the top-priority query variant). The retrieval-plan must include: canonical target title, one-sentence rationale citing the bridge passage index, at least three ordered query variants (first begins with canonical title), the exact literal supporting-text pattern required for final answer (as specified above), explicit bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up query, and a one- or two-sentence justification citing the passage index(es) that motivated the second hop.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The current output identifies the bridge entity but omits an explicit canonical retrieval target, ordered query variants, and the exact literal supporting-text pattern needed to close the hop. This small, localized prompt change makes the next-hop retrieval plan explicit and actionable (so the next agent can fetch the biography and locate the membership statement) while preserving all existing mapping and refusal behavior.

visible_agent_inputs.question: The End of the Innocence was a single by which former member of the Eagles?
passages: The End of the Innocence (song) | "The End of the Innocence" is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album of the same name, released in 1989.  Henley co-wrote and co-produced the song with Bruce Hornsby, who also performed piano; both artists perform the song live in their respective concerts.  Henley's version peaked at number eight on the "Billboard" Hot 100, becoming his fifth solo top ten hit on the chart, more than any of the other members of The Eagles.  "The End of the Innocence" also became his fourth number-one single on the Mainstream Rock Tracks chart and peaked at number two on the Hot Adult Contemporary chart.  The song features Wayne Shorter on saxophone.

The End of the Innocence (album) | The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles.  The album was released in 1989, on Geffen Records, and would be his last release on that label.  It was also his last solo album of the 1980s and it would be eleven years before he released another solo album, until 2000's "Inside Job".

Innocent (Stereophonics song) | "Innocent" is a single by British rock band Stereophonics which was released on 9 November 2009.  It was the first single taken from their seventh studio album, "Keep Calm and Carry On", which was released a week later, on 16 November 2009.  It was also the band's first single for Mercury Records.

Sweet and Innocent (Diamond Head song) | Sweet and Innocent is a single by heavy metal band Diamond Head released in 1980 by Media Records, a Wolverhampton-based label.  It was a single A-side "Streets of Gold" as the B-Side, and was only available on 7".  Both tracks eventually ended up on the re-released version of Diamond Head's 1980 debut Lightning to the Nations in 2001 by Sanctuary Records.

Moving Pictures (band) | Moving Pictures were an Australian rock music band formed in 1978.  Their debut album, "Days of Innocence", was issued in October 1981 and eventually peaked at No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Albums Chart in February the following year.  In January 1982 they released their single, "What About Me", which reached No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Singles Chart.  Late that year Elektra Records issued "Days of Innocence" and "What About Me" in North America.  The single reached No. 29 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and appeared on the associated year-end Hot 100 list for 1983.  A proposed series of United States performances supporting REO Speedwagon, Tom Petty and Hall & Oates fell through when Elektra was substantially reorganised.

The Last Worthless Evening | "The Last Worthless Evening" is a song written by John Corey, Don Henley, and Stan Lynch.  It was a single recorded by Henley in 1989 which reached #21 on US charts.  The song was included on Henley's third album "The End of the Innocence" that same year.
Innocent Chinyoka | Innocent Chinyoka (born 21 June 1982) is a Zimbabwean first-class cricketer who plays for Mashonaland Eagles.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary1

Agent role:
Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

Base prompt p:

1) Format

- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)

  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction

- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)

- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).
